data.title: Firm urges Eagle Pharmaceuticals, Inc. shareholders to discuss rights as officers are being investigated. Contact firm now.
data.description: Investors who own shares in Eagle Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (OTCPK: EGRX) are advised to promptly get in touch with Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm located in New York. The firm is currently looking into potential actions to be taken against specific officers and directors of Eagle Pharmaceuticals, Inc. As time may be limited to exercise shareholder rights, it is crucial for shareholders to contact the firm without delay. This investigation by Halper Sadeh LLC concerns important matters related to the company and can have significant implications for investors.

data.title: Generational Group facilitates sale of Capital Networks Limited to Univerus Media Inc on Dec 10, 2025.-Based in Toronto, offers digital signage solutions globally.
data.description: In Dallas, Generational Group, a prominent mergers and acquisitions advisory firm, has successfully facilitated the sale of Capital Networks Limited to Univerus Media Inc., a division of Univerus Software Inc. This acquisition was finalized on December 10, 2025. Based in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, Capital Networks Limited is a well-established digital signage software company providing communication and content management solutions to organizations globally.

data.title: Wi-Fi 6E & Wi-Fi 7 chipset market report for 2025-2032 shows growth in next-gen wireless tech, aiding digital transformations. - ResearchAndMarkets.com
data.description: In Dublin, the global forecast for the Wi-Fi 6E and Wi-Fi 7 chipset market from 2025 to 2032 has been released, with a report available through ResearchAndMarkets.com. The report highlights how next-generation wireless technologies are transforming enterprise infrastructure to provide robust, high-performance connectivity. The Wi-Fi 6E and Wi-Fi 7 chipset market snapshot showcases the opportunities presented by these advancements.

data.title: r4 Federal won a contract under MDA's SHIELD program worth up to $151 billion for Golden Dome Initiative.
data.description: r4 Federal, a division of r4 Technologies based in Knoxville, Tennessee, has secured a spot as an awardee on the Missile Defense Agency's (MDA) SHIELD contract. The contract is a multiple-award IDIQ contract with an approximate ceiling value of $151 billion, backing the Golden Dome Initiative. The SHIELD program is crucial for MDA's goal to speed up the development and integration of layered defense systems.

data.title: FDA approves Novo Nordisk's Wegovy pill for weight loss, based on OASIS trial demonstrating 16.6% mean weight loss. One-third experienced 20%+ weight loss. First GLP-1 receptor agonist for weight management.
data.description: The FDA has recently given approval for Novo Nordisk A/S' Wegovy pill, which contains once-daily oral semaglutide 25 mg, as a treatment to assist in reducing excess body weight and maintaining long-term weight reduction, as well as lowering the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events. This makes the Wegovy pill the first oral glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonist therapy to be approved for weight management. The approval was granted after reviewing data from the OASIS trial program and the SELECT trial. In the OASIS 4 trial, where oral semaglutide 25 mg was taken once daily, participants with obesity or overweight and one or more comorbidities experienced a mean weight loss of 16.6% when treatment was consistently followed. The weight loss results seen with the Wegovy pill were comparable to those achieved with the injectable Wegovy 2.4 mg. Additionally, one-third of participants in the OASIS 4 trial reported a weight loss of 20% or more. Novo Nordisk is now preparing to launch the Wegovy pill in the market.

data.title: US stocks rise with Dow Jones surging over 200 points, hitting 48673.55; NASDAQ at 23582.81 (+0.09%); S&P 500 at 6927.29 (+0.25%). Initial jobless claims decrease to 214,000, down 10,000 from previous week; Edgewise Therapeutics Inc shares rise by 21%.
data.description: Stocks in the U.S. saw a notable increase in trading, with the Dow Jones index rising by over 200 points midway through the session on Wednesday. The Dow jumped by 0.48% to reach 48,673.55, while the NASDAQ also experienced a slight increase of 0.09%, closing at 23,582.81. The S&P 500 joined the positive trend, advancing by 0.25% to settle at 6,927.29. Noteworthy sector movements included a 0.7% rise in consumer staples shares, counterbalanced by a 0.1% drop in communication services stocks. Among the top headlines was U.S. initial jobless claims decreasing by 10,000 to 214,000 in the week ending Dec. 20, surpassing market projections of 223,000. Edgewise Therapeutics Inc (NASDAQ: EWTX) emerged as a significant gainer, with its shares surging by 21% to $26.36. The company shared updates on the CIRRUS-HCM Phase.

data.title: Trump administration restarts student loan wage garnishment, targeting 1,000 borrowers initially and plans to scale to 5 million in default.
data.description: Starting from the first week of January, the period of leniency for student loans is officially ending. SOFI stock is showing movement - details can be seen in the provided chart and price action. The Trump administration is set to resume wage garnishment for defaulted federal student loan borrowers, initiating the first instance of these "paycheck raids" since the pause due to the pandemic nearly six years ago. Initiated on January 7, 2026, the Department of Education will notify approximately 1,000 defaulted borrowers in the primary wave. Subsequently, month-by-month efforts will be escalated by the Trump administration with a focus on the around 5 million Americans currently in default. Federal law empowers the government to... For the complete article, refer to the source.

data.title: Copper Road closes initial funding round, secured undisclosed amount from investors.
data.description: In recent news, it has been reported that the stock market experienced a significant drop, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average plummeting by over 600 points. This decrease was primarily attributed to concerns over rising inflation rates and potential interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve. Additionally, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq also saw declines of 2.5% and 3.6% respectively. Investors are closely monitoring these developments as they navigate the volatile market conditions. This drop in the market has sparked worries about the overall economic stability and has led to increased uncertainty among traders and analysts. The situation is rapidly evolving, and experts advise caution and strategic decision-making in light of these turbulent market fluctuations.

data.title: 2025 Product of the Year named as personalized accessories like custom stickers and Labubu-style charms, reflecting industry trend.
data.description: In Treovse, Pennsylvania on December 24, 2025, personalized accessories such as custom stickers, pins, and collectible Labubu-style charms have been awarded Counselor's Product of the Year for 2025. This award signifies a significant trend in the promotional products sector towards hyper-personalized items. This recognition highlights the growing preference for customized accessories among consumers.

data.title: Books-A-Million customers and booksellers raised $2.9 million and collected 214,732 books/toys nationwide for holiday drives.
data.description: In Birmingham, Alabama on December 24, 2025, customers and booksellers at Books-A-Million united during the festive period to make a significant charitable impact. Across the nation, Books-A-Million stores amassed 214,732 donated books and toys, along with close to $2.9 million in contributions.

data.title: Holiday spending rises 4.2% year-over-year despite inflation, with technology and personal goods leading. Retailers benefiting.
data.description: Retail companies such as Amazon.com Inc. (NASDAQ: AMZN), Walmart Inc. (NASDAQ: WMT), and Target Corp. (NYSE: TGT) are attracting increased investor attention as early signs indicate that the 2025 holiday shopping season is more robust than anticipated. Despite concerns about inflation and economic instability, U.S. consumers displayed remarkable resilience by boosting retail spending by 4.2% year-over-year. According to preliminary data released on Tuesday by Visa Consulting and Analytics, consumer spending remained firm over a seven-week period starting from November 1, excluding automobile, gasoline, and restaurant purchases. Visa Inc. (NYSE: V) observed a steady increase in spending, especially in technology and personal goods categories, although the figures are not adjusted for inflation. The analysis highlights the dominance of in-store shopping, with online sales contributing significantly to the growth. Retailers are adapting to changing consumer behavior influenced by artificial intelligence technologies. Amazon, in particular, maintained its momentum with plans to employ 250,000 seasonal workers during the holiday period. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: FCC bans Chinese drones, boosting US drone industry. China's DJI faced ban as FCC blocks foreign manufacturers. Domestic drone stocks soar.
data.description: The U.S. drone industry received a significant boost when the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) banned foreign-made uncrewed aircraft systems (UAS) and their critical components, including Chinese drone makers DJI and Autel, from being sold in the country. This decision came after a national security determination on Monday, with the FCC adding these items to its "Covered List." While current drone fleets are still operational, the ban has created a void in the market previously dominated by DJI, which held a market share of nearly 70-80%. This move has led to a surge in domestic drone stocks, and investors are closely monitoring the situation.

data.title: Pudgy Penguins now grace the Las Vegas Sphere following Dogwifhat's failure, showcasing their crypto art.
data.description: The Las Vegas Sphere now features the popular cartoon characters from the crypto-native brand Pudgy Penguins. This unique collaboration has transformed the iconic structure with the beloved creatures. The eye-catching display has garnered attention from locals and tourists alike, with thousands of people stopping by to admire the colorful and whimsical artwork. The partnership between the Las Vegas Sphere and Pudgy Penguins has brought a surge of visitors to the area, with foot traffic increasing by an impressive 30% since the installation of the cartoon creatures. This successful collaboration highlights the growing influence of digital brands in the physical world and the power of merging art with technology to create captivating experiences.
